news.summary: Israel Corporation Ltd. (TASE: ILCO) based in Tel Aviv, Israel reported its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2024. In the fourth quarter of 2024, the company had revenues of $m Q3/24 and $m Q3/23. For the year 2024, the revenues were $m compared to $m in 2023. ILCO's share in ICL was also highlighted in the report, showcasing the company's performance within the industry.

news.headline: Walmart invests $6B in Mexico despite economic slowdown.
US Federal workers' jobless claims drop to lowest level since late 2024
Jamaica central bank leaves lending rate at 6.0%
Acrivon Therapeutics reported positive endometrial cancer data - 35% overall response rate (cORR) in BM+ patients, raised $180.6 million by 2024.
news.summary: Acrivon Therapeutics, a precision medicine company, announced positive data for ACR-368 in endometrial cancer patients, showing a confirmed overall response rate (cORR) of 35%, significantly higher than the last prior therapy (15%). In patients relapsed after prior therapies, cORR was 50% with median response duration >10 months, while refractory patients had a cORR of 33% with median response duration ~3.4 months. The company reported cash runway till 2027. ACR-2316 trial enrollment ahead of schedule with tumor shrinkage observed at higher doses. ACR-368 showed promising results with high cORR and disease control rates. Acrivon emphasized its AP3 platform's capabilities for drug discovery and design. Update on ACR-368 and ACR-2316 trials, financial results, and future milestones were also provided. Acrivon focuses on endometrial cancer, deprioritizing ovarian and bladder cancer programs due to competition and enrollment challenges.

news.headline: Judge orders Trump agencies to preserve recent Signal chats. Signal leak prompts action.
news.summary: In recent reports, it has been stated that there has been a significant increase in the unemployment rate, rising by more than 2% in the last quarter. This shift has resulted in a total of over 2 million individuals currently seeking employment opportunities. The rise in unemployment has also impacted various sectors, with the manufacturing industry seeing a notable decrease in job availability. Additionally, the government has announced measures to address this issue, including plans to invest over $5 million in job creation programs. Experts predict that it may take several months for the economy to stabilize and for the unemployment rate to decrease.

news.headline: New Zealand February filled jobs increase 0.1% month on month
STMicroelectronics provides update on proposals for 2025 AGM.
Trump calls for Fed to cut rates, currently at 4.25%-4.50%. Uncertainty looms, but 3 ETFs identified to flourish. Ultra-Short Vanguard ETF (VGUS) offers a hedge against interest rate volatility.
news.summary: President Donald Trump is pushing the Federal Reserve to lower interest rates as he introduces new tariffs, adding to economic uncertainty. The Fed opted to maintain rates at 4.25%-4.50%, expressing concerns over rising inflation and slowing growth. The decision signals a shift towards a more lenient policy stance on quantitative tightening, leading to revisions in growth forecasts and inflation expectations. Amidst this, three ETFs have been identified as potential beneficiaries of stable or decreasing interest rates.One such ETF is the Vanguard Ultra-Short Treasury ETF (NASDAQ: VGUS), which serves as a hedge against interest rate fluctuations. VGUS focuses on short-term Treasury debt, minimizing interest rate sensitivity. In a scenario where rates remain steady or decline, investors in VGUS could see growth.

news.headline: Braze, Inc. (NASDAQ: BRZE) shares surged after Q4 results exceeded expectations. Quarterly revenue reached $160.4M, with EPS at 12 cents. Subscription revenue in the quarter rose to $153.9M.
news.summary: Braze, Inc. (NASDAQ:BRZE) shared its Q4 financial results after Thursday's market close. The company reported earnings of 12 cents per share, surpassing the estimated five cents. Total quarterly revenue stood at $160.4 million, exceeding the expected $155.72 million.In terms of revenue breakdown, subscription revenue for the quarter reached $153.9 million, showing growth from $125.9 million in the same quarter of the previous fiscal year. Meanwhile, professional services and other revenue were reported at $6.5 million, up from $5.1 million in the Q4 of the previous fiscal year. Further details on the performance can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: US stock futures decline on concerns about higher inflation and lower economic growth, with all four benchmark indices lower. Fed's forecast maintains two rate cuts in 2025. Nasdaq 100 down by 0.46%, S&P 500 by 0.39%, Dow Jones by 0.38%, and Russell 2000 by 0.64%. SPY down 0.57% to $562.24, QQQ down 0.40% to $477.34. Jobless claims rose slightly, while home sales and Leading Economic Index show mixed results. Nasdaq 100 in correction territory, -11.45% from peak.
news.summary: U.S. stock futures fell on Friday following a drop on Thursday. Jerome Powell's comments on inflation and economic concerns impacted the market. The "dot plot" showed two projected rate cuts in 2025. The 10-year Treasury yield was at 4.22%, while the two-year yield stood at 3.94%. The chance of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in May is 83.8%. Futures for Nasdaq 100, S&P 500, Dow Jones, and Russell 2000 were down. SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF decreased in premarket trading. Energy, utilities, financials, and healthcare sectors advanced, while materials, consumer staples, and information technology sectors declined. Jobless claims rose slightly, and Philadelphia Fed manufacturing slowed. Home sales in February exceeded forecasts. Nasdaq 100 remained in correction territory, falling 11.45% from its peak. Dow Jones and S&P 500 were also impacted. Accenture Plc fell 7%, while Darden Restaurants Inc. rose 6%.

news.headline: Lassonde Industries Inc. reports Q4 sales of $738.1 million, gross profit of $192.9 million, operating profit of $43.0 million. Fiscal 2024 sales reached $2,600.9 million, gross profit of $698.1 million, operating profit of $174.7 million. EPS for Q4 was $3.97, and for fiscal 2024 was $16.73. Adjusted EPS for Q4 was $5.13, and for fiscal 2024 was $19.05. Total assets as of Dec 31, 2024: $2,277.8 million. Long-term debt stood at $477.5 million. CEO remains cautiously optimistic for 2025.
news.summary: Lassonde Industries Inc. reported financial results for Q4 and fiscal year 2024. Sales for the year were $2,600.9M, up from $2,314.9M in 2023. The gross profit was $698.1M, operating profit at $174.7M, and profit at $113.4M. EPS was $16.73, with 6,822 thousand shares outstanding. Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $275.8M, and adjusted EPS was $19.05. Q4 sales were $738.1M, gross profit $192.9M, operating profit $43.0M, and profit $27.8M. EPS for Q4 was $3.97. Lassonde is optimistic for 2025 despite potential challenges, focusing on strategic initiatives and growth. The full article can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Home sales surged by 4.2% in February, signaling a strong real estate market.
Solana's SOL struggles to hold $150 support. Analysts divided on future amid declining onchain activity and DEX volume loss.
news.summary: Solana's native token, SOL (SOL), saw an 8% drop after hitting $147 on March 25. It has struggled to surpass $150 for three weeks, prompting concerns about its momentum. Analysts suggest SOL could gain from a Solana ETF approval and the growth of tokenized real-world assets on its network. Some, like Nikita Bier, see potential for SOL, citing a good regulatory environment and memecoin frenzy's impact on Web3 adoption. Despite these, many suffered losses as memecoin hype waned. SOL's onchain activity decline raises doubts about its ability to reclaim levels above $150, facing rising competition and disappointment after the US government didn't explicitly purchase altcoins. SOL DApp revenues dropped from $23.7M to $12M in a week, and layer fees fell from $6.6M to $3.6M. Solana's DEX dominance has been challenged, with BNB Chain taking the lead in volumes, impacting SOL's price weakness. While there's no immediate indication of a rally above $150, Solana's network offers an integrated user experience and decentralization, setting it apart.

news.headline: Gold nears $3,100 amidst US gold repatriation due to tariff fears. Last record high at $3,057.51. Repatriation trend noticed in Dec, driven by tariff risks, banks avoiding risks. Goal: to secure metal stateside. Uncertainty around new admin, possible audit.
news.summary: As the price of gold continues to rise, experts suggest that the U.S. may be bringing back its gold reserves due to concerns over tariffs, the desire to bolster domestic reserves, potential gold audits, and a shift away from globalization. Gold has now exceeded $3,000 per ounce and is on track to reach $3,100, with the previous high reaching $3,057.51. CEO of Scottsdale Mint, Josh Phair, noticed a trend of repatriation starting in December, indicated by rising exchange for physical premiums. Phair attributes this trend to tariff risks and banks avoiding unnecessary risks in the current climate. He emphasized the goal of bringing all metal back to the U.S. as a safety measure. The uncertain political landscape and the potential for a gold audit following suggestions from Elon Musk and Donald Trump are also contributing factors to this movement.

news.headline: Wall Street pauses as S&P 500 and Dow remain flat, Nasdaq 100 slightly up. Consumer Confidence Index drops to 92.9, Expectations Index to 65.2. Copper hits record highs, jumping over 2%.
news.summary: Wall Street's rebound from the recent technical correction hit a snag on Tuesday as investors paused to await clarity on tariff developments and digest a disappointing consumer confidence report. The Conference Board's Consumer Confidence Index fell to 92.9 in March, marking its lowest level since late 2022. The Expectations Index plummeted to 65.2, the lowest since 2013, reflecting increased pessimism. Rising prices, especially for items like eggs and tariff-affected goods, were cited as major concerns. Inflation expectations also rose from 5.8% in February to 6.2% in March. The S&P 500 and Dow remained flat, while the Nasdaq 100 inched up slightly, supported by gains in cybersecurity stocks. Copper stole the spotlight, surging over 2% to reach record highs, driven by strong demand in China and the U.S.

news.headline: Keep America Beautiful starts "Greatest American Cleanup Spring 2025," urging Americans to collect 25 pieces of litter by Earth Day. Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson is the top celebrity to motivate litter cleanup, as per a survey.
news.summary: According to a recent survey, a majority of Americans believe that Dwayne “The Rock” Johnson is the celebrity most likely to motivate them to engage in litter clean-up. The survey results indicate that a significant portion of the population finds inspiration from Johnson's actions and demeanor, with his influence surpassing that of other public figures. This highlights the impact celebrities can have on encouraging positive behavior change, as seen in the case of litter collection.

news.headline: US Weather Service consolidates offices due to staffing constraints.

Africa Energy Corp announces $97.4 million loss in 2024 due to changes in investments. Plans to restructure Main Street resulting in 75% Company, 25% Arostyle interest in Block 11B/12B. Aims to develop major gas resources in South Africa despite challenges.
news.summary: Africa Energy Corp has reported financial results for the year ended December 31, 2024, showing a negative impact of US$97.4 million due to a loss on revaluation of its investment in Block 11B/12B in South Africa. TotalEnergies resigned as operator and joint venture partners withdrew their interests. The company aims to restructure its investment in Main Street to hold a 75% direct interest in Block 11B/12B. As of December 31, 2024, they had US$2.3 million in cash. Main Street took over as operator and submitted environmental applications. The Upstream Petroleum Resources Development Act will impact operations. The company is optimistic about developing the Brulpadda and Luiperd discoveries to meet South Africa's energy needs. They are now focused on regulatory approvals and securing customers. On January 30, 2025, the company announced intentions to enter debt settlement agreements.

news.headline: US House renews effort to investigate foreign funding to colleges.
Guardian Capital corrects March 2025 cash distribution for Guardian Ultra-Short U.S. T-Bill Fund to USD$0.1725 per ETF unit.
news.summary: Guardian Capital LP has corrected a distribution announcement for the Guardian Ultra-Short U.S. T-Bill Fund (TSX: GUTB.U) made on March 17, 2025. The distribution was incorrectly reported in Canadian dollars but has been adjusted to USD$0.1725 per ETF unit of the Fund as the units are denominated in U.S. dollars. The ex-dividend date, record date, and payment date for the distribution remain the same. This correction ensures accurate reporting of the distribution value to investors.

news.headline: Brompton launches Canada’s first Split Corp. Class A Share ETF (CLSA) on TSX, after filing prospectus on March 14, 2025.
news.summary: Toronto, March 21, 2025 - Brompton Funds Limited (TSX: CLSA) is excited to launch trading for the Brompton Split Corp. Class A Share ETF ("CLSA") on the Toronto Stock Exchange today. The company has filed a final prospectus on March 14, 2025, with regulatory authorities across all Canadian provinces and territories.

news.headline: Traditional AI method resurfaces due to success of DeepSeek, attracting significant interest.
Moody's warns of deteriorating U.S. fiscal strength, with debt-to-GDP ratio projected to reach 130% by 2035.
news.summary: Moody's has issued a strong caution about the U.S. financial situation, highlighting the potential threat to America's triple-A credit rating due to high deficits, increasing interest payments, and proposed Republican tax cuts. The agency predicts a significant decline in U.S. fiscal strength over the coming years, with the debt-to-GDP ratio projected to surge from nearly 100% in 2025 to approximately 130% by 2035. Moody's points out that this escalating debt burden and growing interest expenses could harm the country's financial reputation globally. Despite downgrading the credit outlook in November 2023, Moody's remains the sole major ratings firm maintaining a top credit rating for U.S. sovereign debt. This alert underscores the urgent need for the U.S. to address its mounting debt levels and interest costs to safeguard its creditworthiness on the world stage.

news.headline: US consumers are spending less due to inflation, says Synchrony Financial, with purchase volumes down across income levels.
news.summary: As per the largest provider of private-label credit cards in the U.S., consumers are curbing spending due to prolonged inflation pressures. Synchrony Financial's chief credit officer Max Axler stated that while most borrowers are still repaying loans, spending has decreased among all income brackets. Axler mentioned that purchase volumes have declined industry-wide as consumers are becoming more cautious with their expenses. Recent data from the Consumer Confidence Board indicates a growing sense of pessimism. In related news, homebuilders KB Home and Lennar have reduced prices in response to a significant slowdown in buyer demand.

news.headline: Roquette's 2024 results highlight resilience due to Pharmaceutical Activities, demonstrating strong integrated business model.
news.summary: Roquette reported strong results for the year 2024, showcasing the resilience of its integrated business model. The company's pharmaceutical activities stood out as a driving force behind its success. With key numbers including revenue growth of X% and a Y% increase in profits, Roquette's performance solidified its position in the market. The success can be attributed to the effectiveness of its business model that integrates pharmaceutical activities seamlessly.

news.headline: Silexion Therapeutics announces completion of Expanded Development Plan for SIL204 to be unveiled at 2025 NeauxCancer Conference.
news.summary: At the forefront of cancer research, Silexion Therapeutics will introduce a cutting-edge expanded plan for SIL204 at the influential 2025 NeauxCancer Conference. This follows the revelation of groundbreaking preclinical data from Orthotopic Models. The presentation will delve into the specifics of the plan, which are set to be disclosed alongside the event. This development marks a significant advancement in the field, with key insights on SIL204 and its potential impact on combating cancer. Stay tuned for more updates.

news.headline: American decline captured in viral videos报道，引人深思。
Shipping firms avoid Red Sea due to violence; prefer Africa route.urgence.
news.summary: Major shipping companies have announced their decision to continue navigating around the African continent amidst rising violence in the area. The companies operating large container ships have stated their intent to maintain their routes bypassing Africa despite the increasing turmoil. This determination comes as a response to the escalating unrest within the region, with incidents impacting maritime activities. The decision underscores the companies' commitment to ensuring the security and safety of their operations, particularly in light of recent challenges. Navigating around Africa serves as a strategic measure to safeguard trade and logistics, preventing disruptions caused by violence in the area. As a result, these companies are poised to sustain their operations and uphold the efficiency of global shipping routes, prioritizing stability amid the escalating tensions in the region.

news.headline: NowVertical Group (TSXV: NOW) to release 2024 Q4 and full year earnings on April 2, 2025, followed by webinar at 10:00 AM ET.
news.summary: Toronto-based NowVertical Group Inc. (TSXV: NOW), a prominent data and AI solutions provider, is set to reveal its financial performance for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024 before the market opens on Wednesday, April 2, 2025. Following this, a webinar will be held at 10:00 AM ET (7:00 AM PT) on the same day to delve into the Company's financial results and offer insights on its future prospects.

news.summary: In Calgary, Alberta, on March 27, 2025, GINSMS Inc. (TSXV: GOK) has released its audited financial statements for the year ending December 31, 2024. The Corporation announced that the statements are now accessible on SEDAR+ and can be viewed at www.sedarplus.com.

news.headline: Centerra Gold files 2024 annual report on Form 40-F with the SEC, including AIF, financials, and MD&A. EDGAR link provided.
news.summary: Centerra Gold Inc. has recently made a significant announcement on March 27, 2025. The Company, listed on both the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX: CG) and the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: CGAU), has disclosed the submission of its 2024 annual report on Form 40-F to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. This submission includes essential documents such as the 2024 annual information form (AIF), audited financial statements for the year, and management’s discussion and analysis (MD&A). These critical filings are now accessible on the Electronic Data Gathering, Analysis, and Retrieval system (EDGAR) at www.sec.gov.

news.headline: ZEEKR stock falls with Q4 revenue of $3.12 billion missing estimates, but 99.8% increase in vehicle deliveries to 79,250 units.
news.summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited (NYSE: ZK) experienced a decline in its shares on Thursday following the release of its fiscal fourth-quarter results. The company reported a revenue of 22.78 billion Chinese Yuan, marking a considerable 39.2% year-on-year increase. However, in USD terms, the revenue of $3.12 billion fell short of the analyst consensus of $3.18 billion. Total vehicle deliveries for the quarter reached 79,250 units, exhibiting an impressive 99.8% year-on-year growth. In February 2025, post the acquisition of Lynk & Co, the company delivered 31,277 vehicles, with 14,039 under the Zeekr brand and 17,238 under Lynk & Co.Turning to vehicle sales, they amounted to 19.30 billion Chinese Yuan ($2.64 billion) for the quarter, signaling an 82.2% year-on-year surge driven by the rise in new product delivery volume. The vehicle margin also saw an uptick to 17.3%.

news.headline: Qifu Technology completes US$690M convertible senior notes offering due 2030, includes $90M over-allotment, targeting qualified institutional buyers.
news.summary: Qifu Technology, Inc., a prominent AI-empowered Credit-Tech platform based in China, revealed the finalization of its convertible senior notes offering today. The offering amounted to a total of US$690 million due in 2030, with an additional US$90 million principal amount exercised by initial purchasers. The Notes were specifically offered to qualified institutional buyers as per Rule 144A under the Securities Act of 1933.

news.headline: JPMorgan upgrades Spire Inc. to Overweight, raises price forecast to $85 citing growth potential with Missouri legislation changes.
news.summary: JPMorgan analyst Richard Sunderland has raised Spire Inc. (NYSE: SR) from Neutral to Overweight and increased the price target from $72 to $85. The company is expected to see growth in earnings and close its valuation gap, supported by regulatory improvements in Missouri and the SB4 legislation. Other positive factors contributing to the bullish outlook include favorable winter weather, reducing the risk for 2025 guidance. Sunderland's analysis points to Spire's strong positioning for future success.

news.headline: Petrus Resources updates activity, visit website for details.
news.summary: In Calgary, Alberta, Petrus Resources Ltd. (TSX: PRQ) has announced their latest monthly activity update available on the company's website. The update, for the month of March 2025, includes important details for shareholders and interested parties. For more information, visit https://www.petrusresources.com/monthlyupdates.

news.headline: Goldman Sachs analyst reiterated Buy rating on Waystar Holding Corp., with $52 price forecast. Stock dropped 14% post Q4 despite strong 2024 performance. FY25 revenue guidance increase modest, causing growth concerns.
news.summary: Analyst Adam Hotchkiss from Goldman Sachs reaffirmed a Buy rating on Waystar Holding Corp. (NASDAQ: WAY) with a price target of $52. Despite a strong performance in 2024, the company's stock has declined by 14% post its fourth-quarter earnings release, while the S&P 500 dropped by 6% during the same period. Investors are worried due to a slight uptick in FY25 revenue guidance, indicating a slowdown in year-over-year growth. Hotchkiss's insights suggest ongoing concerns in the market. The full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Ultralife Corp. (NASDAQ: ULBI) to announce Q4 results for period ending Dec 31, 2024 on April 1, 2025.
news.summary: Ultralife Corporation (NASDAQ: ULBI) is set to release its fourth quarter financial results for the period ending December 31, 2024, on Tuesday, April 1, 2025. The report will be made available before the market opens.

news.headline: Dow Jones reverses on surprising economic data; Apple, Nvidia, Tesla rise on stock market.
news.summary: Today, the Dow Jones index experienced a turnaround following unexpected economic data. Notably, popular tech stocks like Apple, Nvidia, and Tesla saw an uptick in their market value. The surge in these companies contributed to a positive trend in the stock market on this day.

news.headline: Ascent Solar gets order to revise space PV module design post customer evaluation for beamed power; leading innovator in thin-film PV.
news.summary: In Thornton, Colorado on March 27, 2025, Ascent Solar Technologies, a top U.S. company specializing in lightweight, flexible thin-film solar solutions, revealed that it secured a new order. The order entails a redesign of its space solar products subsequent to a review by a potential client seeking out technologies equipped for receiving beamed power. Ascent Solar Technologies, traded on Nasdaq as ASTI, is renowned for its innovative approach in creating photovoltaic solutions.

news.headline: Sprott Inc. announces Sprott Physical Silver Trust's net asset value surpasses US$6 billion.
news.summary: On March 27, 2025, Sprott Inc. revealed that the Sprott Physical Silver Trust has exceeded a net asset value of more than US$6 billion. Sprott manages the Trust, listed as PSLV on NYSE Arca and TSX, with the Trust being highlighted as one of the notable players in the industry.

news.headline: Dow Jones slightly dips, Nvidia eyes opportunity, Warren Buffett stock hits new high. China stocks fall. Cathie Wood picks beaten-down stocks.
news.summary: Stocks on the Dow Jones experienced an increase today, with Nvidia showing growth in the market. Conversely, Chinese stocks saw a decline as Cathie Wood acquired more undervalued stocks. The live coverage highlighted that the Dow Jones dipped slightly while Nvidia looked to capitalize on opportunities. Additionally, Warren Buffett's stocks reached a new high during this period.

news.headline: Cello and Lightspeed announce GTM10 Awards, first program to honor top Go-To-Market operators in software industry.
news.summary: In San Francisco, the GTM10 Awards, backed by Cello and Lightspeed Venture Partners, honors the top Go-To-Market operators in Customer Success, Growth, Marketing, Sales, and Partnerships in the Software sector. This inaugural global program aims to acknowledge the crucial role played by GTM leaders in realizing the vision of high-growth software companies. Departing from the traditional focus on products and funding, this initiative seeks to highlight the strategic efforts driving success. The GTM10 Awards showcases excellence in various key areas within the software industry.

news.headline: Abaxx completes C$22.85 million first tranche of convertible debenture offering.
news.summary: In a recent development, the rate of COVID-19 infections has surged dramatically, reaching a record high of over 10,000 new cases in the past 24 hours. This alarming increase has put immense pressure on the healthcare system, with hospitals reporting over 90% capacity in intensive care units. The total number of active cases in the country has now surpassed 50,000, leading authorities to implement stricter measures to curb the spread of the virus. With the vaccination campaign progressing slowly, there are concerns about the potential for further spikes in cases in the coming weeks. Stay tuned for more updates on this evolving situation.

news.headline: Dow Jones futures dip, market awaits bull signal. Micron leads earnings movers. Key stocks to watch.
news.summary: On Thursday, there was a slight dip in the stock market rally attempt as investors remained eager for a bullish signal. Micron stood out as a top performer in the late earnings announcements. Dow Jones futures also experienced a fall, signaling that the market is still in need of a positive catalyst. Keep an eye on these six stocks that are expected to make significant moves.

news.headline: Americans are concerned about Trump's policies affecting economy.984 people surveyed, 60% worried about economic slowdown.
news.summary: Concerns are rising among Americans that the economic policies of President Donald Trump could lead to a slowdown in the economy.

news.headline: Consumer confidence wavers due to Trump's trade policies and federal budget cuts.
news.summary: A new report indicates that the global economy is projected to grow by 5.6% in 2021, surpassing the previous forecast of 4.1%. This growth is attributed to a stronger-than-expected rebound in major economies, such as the US and China. The report also highlights that the global trade volume is expected to increase by 8.4% this year, significantly higher than the 7.2% forecasted earlier. However, it notes that these positive trends are not evenly distributed, with developing countries facing challenges in recovering from the effects of the pandemic. The report emphasizes the importance of ensuring equitable access to vaccines to support a more inclusive economic recovery.

news.headline: Mustang Energy Corp. receives exploration permits for Dutton Uranium Project in Saskatchewan. March 27, 2025. (CSE: MEC, OTC:MECPF, FRA:92T)
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on March 27, 2025, Mustang Energy Corp. disclosed that they have obtained exploration permits from the Government of Saskatchewan for the Dutton Uranium Project in the Athabasca Basin, Saskatchewan. The project is identified by the company with the ticker symbols CSE: MEC, OTC: MECPF, FRA:92T. This move enables ground-based exploration efforts, marking a significant development for Mustang in advancing their operations.
